2 of 6

From tutoring to advising: what belongs in the model vs. the application layer

A framework for deciding which AI capabilities should be trained into the model versus engineered into the surrounding system—optimizing for quality, safety, and sustainability

Application Layer
Governed System
Base Model
Trained AI Intelligence
Knowledge Graphs
External Graph DB + RAG
Houses dynamic knowledge constructs with clear provenance and permission controls
Reasoning Engine
Trained to reason over graph structures (prereqs, misconceptions) and use tools to query data
Student Model
Persistent Learner State
Manages mastery, goals, and accommodations for privacy; explicitly linked to the KG
Adaptive Tutor
Interprets state to adapt behavior; fine-tuned for "tutoring moves" using annotated transcripts
Standards & Curriculum
Authoritative Mappings
Structured overlays on the KG; owns dynamic curriculum and course mappings
Contextual Behavior
Reasons its moves based on standards provided in context, rather than internalizing them
Authoring & Grading
System Controls
Manages rubrics, item banks, and policies; provides auditable, human-reviewable grading
Creative Assistant
Assists with item generation and feedback aligned with rubrics via fine-tuning on many examples
Student Advising
LMS/SIS + Policy Layer
Secure access to enrollment, progress, program rules, and financial aid with permissions and human oversight
Advising Assistant
Reasons over data to explain options, surface pathways and trade-offs; never makes autonomous high-stakes decisions
Key Insight
High-stakes decisions and dynamic data stay in the system layer; pedagogical intelligence and adaptive reasoning live in the model.
Model assists; system decides on high-stakes matters
Previous: AI Tutoring Architecture
Next: Learnvia SDK App
3 of 6

The Impact-Scale Trade-off: Course Infrastructure Drives Impact, Limits Scale

Full course context (instructor, grading, timeline) maximizes learning gains; extracted components integrated into Learnvia SDK App reach more learners with less impact

Course + Courseware: The Closed Learning Loop

Full Learnvia achieves +10pp impact because course infrastructure - instructor, grading, and timeline - enables all five loop steps.

The Learnvia SDK App preserves TEACH and PRACTICE but loses ASSESS without grades. Base LLM keeps TEACH but little else.

Loop Score
5/5
2.5/5
1/5
5/5 = +10pp  |  2.5/5 = +2pp  |  1/5 = +1pp
DIAGNOSE: Find knowledge gaps before teaching  |  TEACH: Deliver right content at right level  |  PRACTICE: Scaffolded tasks with Socratic guidance  |  ASSESS: Mastery-based evaluation  |  REFINE: Adapt based on what's working

Scale Out: Conditions of Success
SDK App Deployment & Usability
Reduce friction: enable, select, trigger flow. Students must discover and activate easily.
LLM Leadership Alignment
Building capabilities we jointly agree OpenAI, Anthropic, Google need for education.
Technical Architecture
Integration-ready design for LLM/Chat level. Feature architecture validated by top LLM Architects for future embedding.
Right Student Environment
Target students without advanced courseware. Avoid competing with Learnvia itself.
Evidence of Efficacy
RCT validation required before institutions will recommend to students at scale.
Institutional IT Approval
Enterprise ChatGPT Edu deployments may restrict available GPTs/tools.
Content Coverage Breadth
Gateway math is limited. Might need expansion to more courses for meaningful scale.
Data Privacy Compliance
FERPA/GDPR implications for portable student profiles across platforms.
Business Model Clarity
Who pays for SDK access? Free to students vs. institution license affects adoption path and sustainability.
Key Risk: Each unmet condition limits scale ceiling. Parallel workstreams needed across technical, partnership, and policy tracks.
Source: Learnvia research data; Gates Foundation analysis
Previous: Impact x Scale Equation
Next: MLE Infrastructure

Beyond Generic AI: Student & Faculty Use Cases
Maria
| College Algebra

"I'm stuck on another factoring problem..."

Student Model AI Tutoring
ChatGPT Treats as fresh question. Tomorrow she's back.
Learnvia SDK App Flags "4th struggle this week." Pivots to fundamentals.
Outcome Masters fundamentals. Progresses.
DeShawn
| Calc 2, Midnight

"I've watched 5 videos on integration by parts..."

Knowledge Graphs AI Tutoring
ChatGPT Explains it a 6th way. Real issue: never mastered u-sub.
Learnvia SDK App KG identifies prerequisite gap. Fixes root cause.
Outcome Finally gets it. Passes midterm.
Dr. Chen
| Faculty, Calc 1

"I need to create lecture content on limits..."

Content Creation Best Practices
ChatGPT Generic explanations. No scaffolding or worked examples.
Learnvia SDK App Applies cognitive load theory. Faded worked examples.
Outcome Research-backed content. 40% less prep time.
Prof. Martinez
| Faculty, Stats

"I need a problem set on hypothesis testing..."

Homework Creation Best Practices
ChatGPT Random difficulty. No sequencing or spaced practice.
Learnvia SDK App Interleaved practice. Prerequisite-aware sequencing.
Outcome Higher retention. Students prepared for exams.
Source: Investment Plan; Student scenarios from gateway math research; 7 courses: Pre-calc, Calc 1-3, College Algebra, Quant Reasoning, Intro Stats

GF Can Deliver What AI Labs Need: Real-World Learning Validation (MLE)

Validating Learnvia SDK's learning science in real classrooms creates the evidence AI Labs need and would be challenging to replicate.

The Benchmark Gap
Current AI benchmarks measure the wrong thing
MMLU, MATH, GSM8K test answer accuracy, not whether students actually learn
High Benchmark Scores
/=
Real Learning Outcomes
"AI labs know their tools underperform in real classrooms"
GF's Unique Asset
What AI Labs cannot build themselves
Learnvia's proven learning science (Knowledge Graph, Student Model, AI Tutoring)
Real classrooms with real outcomes (HBCUs, CCs)
Longitudinal tracking through graduation
Independent, equity-focused credibility
Portfolio Evaluation
Tiered evidence: Learnvia SDK vs. generic AI
Weeks
Rapid Studies
SDK components vs. baseline AI
Semester
Instructor Studies
Same instructor, SDK integration
Semester
Section RCTs
Multi-section, causal evidence
Years
Longitudinal
Track SDK impact through completion
Building evidence that SDK learning science works

Specialized engineering and MLE teams for rigorous efficacy validation

30-Month Phased Roadmap
PLAN + BUILD 24 months
Q2 2026 - Q2 2028
  • Partner engagement
  • Capability development
  • SDK creation
Mid 2028
VALIDATE
2028-2029
  • MLE Studies
  • Semester and Longitudinal
SCALE or EXIT
2030+
  • Go/no-go decision
  • If validated: open-source release
GO/NO-GO GATE: RCT must demonstrate +2pp completion improvement to proceed to open-source release
Strategic Pillars: Next Steps
1
LearnVia SDK App
  • - Define SDK capabilities and architecture (PRD)
  • - Identify core capabilities R&D partners
  • - Identify Integration partner
2
MLE Planning
  • - Build benchmark-to-real-world comparison framework
  • - Recruit HBCU, TCU, and Community College validation partners
3
AI Labs Engagement
  • - Share SDK roadmap to influence AI product development for learning
  • - Develop "Benchmark Gap" value proposition for AI lab partnerships
  • - Propose joint validation pilot with at least 1 AI lab by Q1 2027

ALT 1 of 5

Two Paths to 40K Completions: High Impact at Moderate Scale vs. Massive Scale at Lower Impact

Both approaches converge on similar total outcomes today--revealing an opportunity to combine their strengths

Learnvia: AI-Enabled Full Closed Loop

Adds context into learning interactions through integration with learner profiles, faculty data, standards, knowledge graphs, and curriculum alignment

Step Learnvia Capabilities
● DIAGNOSE
Learner profile integrates with interaction
Faculty receives real-time performance data
● TEACH
Content aligned to knowledge graph & standards
Adapts to diagnosed learner state
● PRACTICE
Questions tailored to learner profile
Feedback flows back to diagnosis
● ASSESS
Formal, graded assessments
Results integrated into learner model
● REFINE
Faculty trained on Learnvia data
Receives actionable outcome feedback
Loop Score 5/5
+10pp
(target)
400K learners
40K completions
The Convergence
Learnvia AI Capabilities

The closed-loop architecture is powered by these core AI capabilities that enable personalized learning at scale

Capability Description
KNOWLEDGE GRAPH Concept map with prerequisite links
STUDENT MODEL KG of learner's mastery and gaps
AI AUTHORING Authoring using Learnvia standards
AI TUTORING Scaffolding, Homework optimized
AI SAFETY Dealing with Mental Health Crisis
STATE STANDARDS... Mapping between standards frameworks
Impact × Scale

Both paths converge on similar outcomes today--architecture, not raw capability, determines learning impact

Learnvia: 10pp x 400K = ~40K
Base LLM: 1pp x 4.25M = ~42K
~40K completions each
ASK
How can we take advantage of both paths to impact?
ANSWER
Validate which closed-loop elements transfer to LLM scale
Base LLM: Partial Loop

General AI capability with limited persistent learner context; interactions are mostly session-bound with no integration to institutional data or faculty workflows

Step Base LLM Capabilities
○ DIAGNOSE
No persistent learner profile
No faculty visibility
◐ TEACH
General knowledge (no curriculum alignment)
Natural language explanations
No learner context integration
◐ PRACTICE
Session-only memory
Generic problem assistance
No profile-based personalization
○ ASSESS
No formal assessment integration
No outcome tracking
○ REFINE
Faculty unaware of student LLM use
Cannot align instruction
Loop Score 1/5
+1pp
(hypothetical)
4.25M learners
42K completions
Note: Impact estimates are hypothetical targets. The gap between +1pp and +10pp reflects learning architecture, not AI capability. | Gates Foundation DETL AI Sandbox

Validation Pathway
1
Build SDK
Learnvia Lite SDK
2
Test (Portfolio)
Pilot: 200-500
Quasi-exp: 2K-5K

Target: +2pp
Go/No-Go decision

OpenAI, Anthropic,
Google as customers
Conditions for Impact
  • •
    Right learners and use cases - Targets students lacking advanced courseware without competing with Learnvia
  • •
    Proven learning outcomes - MLE evidence required for institutional endorsement
  • •
    Meaningful scope with compliant data use - Sufficient content breadth with FERPA compliance
Conditions for Scale
  • •
    Frictionless deployment - Easy discovery, activation, and use within SDK and enterprise environments
  • •
    Platform and architecture alignment - Built in alignment with major LLM providers and validated for future integration
  • •
    Institutional and commercial viability - Works within IT constraints and has a clear, sustainable business model
Portfolio validation approach: Rapid iteration --> signal detection --> selective rigorous evaluation | All validation will disaggregate by student demographics | Gates Foundation DETL AI Sandbox
